user.title: Samsung TV Smart - Authentication
user.description:
Choose how to authenticate with SmartThings.

OAuth Status: {oauth_status}

pick_implementation.title: Sign in with Samsung
pick_implementation.description: Select your SmartThings account to authenticate.

host.title: Configure TV
host.description: Enter your Samsung TV's IP address and a name for the device.

data.name: Device Name
data.use_ha_name_for_ws: Use Home Assistant location name for WebSocket

manual.title: Manual Configuration
manual.description:
Enter your TV's IP address and SmartThings credentials.

To create a Personal Access Token, visit {st_tokens_url}

data.name: Device Name
data.use_ha_name_for_ws: Use Home Assistant location name for WebSocket

st_integration.title: Use SmartThings Integration
st_integration.description: Reuse the authentication from your existing SmartThings integration.

stdevice.title: Select SmartThings Device
stdevice.description: Multiple Samsung TVs found. Please select your TV:

stdeviceid.title: SmartThings Device ID
stdeviceid.description:
No TV found automatically. Please enter the SmartThings Device ID manually.

You can find this in the SmartThings app or developer portal.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Samsung TV
reconfigure.description: Choose what to change for this TV.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authentication Required
reauth_confirm.description: Your SmartThings authentication has expired. Click Submit to sign in again.

reconfigure_connection.title: Connection
reconfigure_connection.description: Update the TV's IP address and WebSocket port. Use 8001 unless your TV only answers on the SSL port 8002.

reconfigure_auth.title: Authentication
reconfigure_auth.description: Pick how Home Assistant authenticates with this TV. For OAuth2, just select it and submit — the login flow starts automatically (the fields below are ignored). For PAT, paste a SmartThings Personal Access Token. For SmartThings Integration, pick an existing entry.

reconfigure_ip_control.title: IP Control
reconfigure_ip_control.description:
IP Control gives reliable power on/off (even from Art Mode) without SmartThings. Current status: {status}.

Check "Pair now" to (re)pair the TV — it must be ON in normal viewing with IP Remote enabled. The toggles below appear once paired.

data.ip_control_art_mode: Use IP Control for Art Mode

reconfigure_ip_pair.title: Pair IP Control
reconfigure_ip_pair.description:
Pairs the TV's IP Control interface.

Before continuing:
1. The TV must be ON and in NORMAL viewing — not Art Mode.
2. Enable IP Remote: Settings → All Settings → Connections → Network → Expert Settings → Enable IP Remote.

Accept the authorization prompt on the TV when it appears. Current status: {status}.

error.invalid_host: Unable to connect to the TV. Check the IP address and ensure the TV is on.
error.only_key_or_st: Please provide either a Personal Access Token OR select a SmartThings integration, not both.
error.st_api_key_fail: Could not retrieve the API key from the SmartThings integration.
error.wrong_api_key: The SmartThings token is invalid. Please check and try again.
error.st_device_not_found: No Samsung TV found with this SmartThings Device ID.
error.st_device_used: This SmartThings device is already configured.
error.not_successful: Could not connect to the TV. Ensure it's on and allow the connection when prompted.
error.ip_control_pair_failed: IP Control pairing failed. Make sure the TV is ON in normal viewing (not Art Mode) and IP Remote is enabled in Settings, then try again.
error.ip_control_no_host: No TV host is configured for this entry.

abort.unsupported_version: This integration requires Home Assistant {req_ver} or later. You are running {run_ver}.
abort.host_unique_id: Cannot reconfigure: The TV's unique ID is based on the host address.
abort.oauth_failed: OAuth authentication failed. Please try again.
abort.oauth_not_configured:
OAuth2 is not configured.

Please add SmartThings credentials in:
**Settings → Devices & Services → Application Credentials**
abort.no_smartthings_integration: No SmartThings integration found. Please set up SmartThings first or use another authentication method.
abort.missing_scopes: The granted permissions are insufficient. Please ensure you grant all requested permissions.
abort.reauth_account_mismatch: The account used for re-authentication doesn't match the original account.
abort.ip_control_pair_successful: IP Control paired successfully. The access token has been stored.

sync_ent.title: Synchronized Entities
sync_ent.description: Select entities to synchronize with TV power state.

data.use_mute_check: Use Mute State for Power Detection

art_identify.title: Art identification
art_identify.description: Identify the artwork shown on the Frame TV via reverse image search (Google Vision) confirmed by an LLM. All fields are optional except the toggle; leave a key blank to keep the previously saved one.

error.invalid_tv_list: Invalid list format. Please use valid JSON format.
error.ip_control_pair_failed: Pairing failed. Make sure the TV is on and in normal viewing (not Art Mode), and that IP Remote is enabled, then try again.
error.ip_control_no_host: No host address is configured for this TV.

application_credentials.description:
To use OAuth2 authentication with Samsung TV Smart, you need to create SmartThings developer credentials.

**Setup Instructions:**

1. Go to [SmartThings Developer Portal]({smartthings_portal})
2. Sign in with your Samsung account
3. Click **New Project** → Select **Automation for the Home**
4. Give your project a name (e.g., 'Home Assistant TV')
5. Click **Register App** in the sidebar
6. Select **OAuth2 / Credentials**
7. Fill in the form:
   - **App Name**: Home Assistant Samsung TV
   - **Redirect URI**: `{callback_url}`
   - **Scopes**: Select `r:devices:*` and `x:devices:*`
8. Click **Save** and copy your credentials below

[SmartThings OAuth Documentation]({oauth_docs})

oauth2_implementation_unavailable.message: OAuth2 credentials are not configured. Please add SmartThings credentials in Settings → Devices & Services → Application Credentials.

oauth_auth_failed.title: SmartThings authentication failed for {device_name}
oauth_auth_failed.description:
Home Assistant could not refresh the SmartThings access token for **{device_name}**. The refresh token is no longer valid — this can happen when the token is revoked, expires, or is rotated by SmartThings.

SmartThings-based features (power state, picture mode, source selection) will not work until you reconnect.

To fix this, go to **Settings → Devices & Services → Samsung TV Smart**, select the affected entry, choose **Reconfigure**, and sign in with Samsung again.
